engineIntelligence.h2: Porsche Boxster Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Porsche Boxster in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Porsche Boxster M96.21 engine - petrol 3.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: IMS bearing failure - intermediate shaft bearing wear can cause catastrophic engine failure from 60k miles.
Cylinder bore scoring - Nikasil liner wear on high-mileage units leads to compression loss and oil consumption.

cards.altText: Porsche Boxster M97.21 engine - petrol 3.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - plastic guides degrade, causing rattles and potential chain skip on higher-mileage units.
Rear main seal leaks - oil seepage from crankshaft seal common after 70k miles, requiring engine removal to address.

cards.altText: Porsche Boxster MA1.22 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger wastegate rattle - common on early-production units, may require turbo replacement or refurbishment.
Direct injection carbon buildup - intake valve deposits can cause rough idle and reduced power on high-mileage examples.

engineGuide.h2: Porsche Boxster Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Porsche Boxster across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level flat-six for the original 986 Boxster; aluminium construction but IMS bearing vulnerability defined its replacement legacy.
commonFailures: IMS bearing wear - intermediate shaft bearing failure risk from 60k miles, can cause catastrophic damage.
Rear main seal leaks - oil seepage common on higher-mileage units, requires engine removal to address.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M96.20 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Performance flagship for 986 generation; increased displacement and power but retained IMS bearing design that drives UK replacement demand.
commonFailures: IMS bearing failure - most documented failure on M96.21, catastrophic if not addressed preventatively.
Cylinder bore scoring - Nikasil liner wear on high-mileage examples leads to compression loss.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M96.21 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-range 986 engine replacing the 2.5L; improved torque but still susceptible to IMS and bore scoring issues common to M96 family.
commonFailures: IMS bearing wear - same vulnerability as other M96 codes, preventative upgrade recommended.
Rear main seal leaks - oil leaks from crankshaft seal common after 70k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M96.22 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Petrol (Facelift)
entries.history: Revised M96.22 for 986 facelift; minor updates but core architecture and failure patterns unchanged from pre-facelift units.
commonFailures: IMS bearing vulnerability - still present despite minor revisions, failure risk remains.
Bore scoring potential - cylinder liner wear documented on higher-mileage examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M96.23 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol (Facelift)
entries.history: Updated M96.21 for 986 facelift; retained performance credentials but IMS bearing remained the dominant replacement driver for UK owners.
compatibleVariants: Boxster S 3.2 (986 Facelift), Boxster S 3.2 Tiptronic (986 Facelift)
commonFailures: IMS bearing failure - highest-volume replacement trigger for M96.24 in UK marketplace.
Cylinder bore scoring - documented on high-mileage examples, can necessitate full rebuild.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M96.24 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

entries.title: 2.9L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level engine for 987.2 generation; direct injection introduced but reliability improved over M96, reducing replacement frequency.
commonFailures: Direct injection carbon buildup - intake valve deposits can cause rough idle on high-mileage units.
Timing chain tensioner wear - less common than M96 but documented on higher-mileage examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M97.20 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

entries.title: 3.4L Petrol
entries.history: Performance engine for early 987 Boxster S; increased displacement and power with revised timing system but chain tensioner issues persist.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - plastic guides can degrade, causing rattles and potential chain skip.
Rear main seal leaks - oil seepage from crankshaft seal common after 70k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M97.21 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

entries.title: 3.4L Petrol (GTS)
entries.history: Enhanced M97.21 for Boxster GTS; sport-tuned output and components but shares core architecture and known failure patterns with standard S.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - same vulnerability as M97.21, preventative inspection recommended.
Direct injection carbon buildup - intake valve deposits can affect performance on high-mileage units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M97.22 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

entries.title: 3.8L Petrol (Spyder)
entries.history: Limited-run high-output engine for Boxster Spyder; rare, high-value replacement with specialist rebuild requirements and premium pricing.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - same as other M97 codes but rarity increases replacement complexity.
High-performance component wear - sport-tuned parts may require specialist sourcing during rebuild.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M97.24 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: First turbocharged flat-four for 718 Boxster; downsized displacement with forced induction delivering strong performance but new failure patterns.
commonFailures: Turbocharger wastegate rattle - common on early-production units, may require turbo refurbishment.
Direct injection carbon buildup - intake valve deposits can cause rough idle on high-mileage examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for MA1.22 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: Performance turbo flat-four for 718 Boxster S; increased displacement and output with same turbo architecture and failure considerations as MA1.22.
compatibleVariants: 718 Boxster S 2.5 Turbo, 718 Boxster S 2.5 Turbo PDK, 718 Boxster GTS 4.0 (note: GTS 4.0 uses different engine)
commonFailures: Turbocharger wastegate rattle - same vulnerability as MA1.22, documented on early-production units.
Direct injection carbon buildup - intake valve deposits can affect performance on higher-mileage examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for MA1.23 Porsche Boxster engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Porsche Boxster Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Porsche Boxster's flat-six engines deliver iconic performance but face well-documented challenges, particularly IMS bearing failure on M96 units and timing chain issues on M97. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the problem groups below to understand your options and costs.

problems.h4: IMS Bearing Failure - Why M96 Porsche Boxster Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The intermediate shaft bearing uses a sealed design that can degrade over time, allowing metal particles to enter the engine and cause catastrophic damage if the bearing fails completely.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative IMS Upgrade - Replace bearing before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove gearbox, install upgraded open-bearing IMS kit with improved seals
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; viable if caught before symptoms appear, extends engine life significantly

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M96 Engine - Address catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded IMS, new seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when failure has occurred; upgraded components address original weak point

problems.recommendation: If your M96 Boxster is under 80k miles with no symptoms, a preventative IMS upgrade from a specialist offers excellent value. If you're hearing rattles or have confirmed failure, replacement with a rebuilt unit featuring upgraded internals is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M96 engine prices for your Porsche Boxster

problems.h4: Cylinder Bore Scoring - Why High-Mileage Boxster Engines Lose Compression
problems.rootCause: The Nikasil-coated aluminium cylinder bores can develop scoring from piston ring wear or inadequate lubrication, leading to compression loss, increased oil consumption, and eventual engine failure.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Bore Inspection & Liner Repair - Address early scoring
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, inspect bores, install steel liners if scoring is limited to one or two cylinders
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if scoring is minimal; not suitable for widespread damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M96 with Liners - Comprehensive solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with steel liners fitted to all cylinders, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for confirmed scoring; addresses root cause and restores reliability

problems.recommendation: Bore scoring is progressive - early detection via borescope inspection can save money. If scoring is confirmed across multiple cylinders, a rebuilt engine with steel liners is the only reliable long-term solution for your Boxster.
problems.cta: Get quotes for bore-scoring repair on your Porsche Boxster engine

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - M97 Boxster S Reliability Concern
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain tensioner guides can degrade over time, causing chain slack, rattling noises, and in severe cases, chain skip that can damage valves and pistons.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Tensioner Replacement - Address wear before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove ancillaries, replace tensioners and guides with updated components
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; viable if caught early, extends timing system life

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M97 Engine - Address catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated timing components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when damage has occurred; upgraded parts address original weak point

problems.recommendation: If your M97.21 Boxster S is making timing chain rattles, act immediately. A preventative tensioner replacement is cost-effective if caught early; if damage has occurred, a rebuilt engine with updated components is the reliable path forward.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M97.21 engine prices for your Porsche Boxster S

problems.h4: Turbocharger Wastegate Rattle - 718 Boxster Turbo Concern
problems.affectedModels: Porsche 718 Boxster 2.0 Turbo, 718 Boxster S 2.5 Turbo (2016-2020, MA1.22/MA1.23)
problems.rootCause: Early-production turbochargers can develop wastegate actuator play, causing a metallic rattling noise on deceleration and potentially affecting boost control and performance.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Refurbishment - Address wastegate play
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, refurbish wastegate actuator, rebalance, reinstall with updated seals
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for early rattles; restores boost control and eliminates noise

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt MA1 Turbo Engine - Address catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated turbo components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure has caused engine damage; upgraded parts address weak points

problems.recommendation: A wastegate rattle on your 718 Boxster should be inspected promptly. If caught early, turbo refurbishment is cost-effective. If the turbo has failed and caused engine damage, a rebuilt unit with updated components is the reliable solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 718 Boxster turbo engine replacement

problems.h4: Rear Main Seal Leaks - Common Oil Leak on High-Mileage Boxsters
problems.rootCause: The rear main crankshaft seal can harden and shrink over time, allowing oil to seep from the engine/gearbox junction, leading to low oil levels and potential engine damage if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - RMS Replacement - Address leak before damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove gearbox, replace rear main seal with updated component, refill oil
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if leak is caught early; prevents oil loss and potential engine damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M96 Engine - Address catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new seals throughout, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil loss has caused internal damage; upgraded seals address original weak point

problems.recommendation: An oil leak at the engine/gearbox junction on your Boxster should be inspected immediately. If caught early, RMS replacement is cost-effective. If oil loss has caused internal damage, a rebuilt engine is the reliable path forward.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M96 engine prices for your Porsche Boxster

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Porsche Boxster
finalCta.paragraph: From IMS bearing concerns on classic 986 models to turbo wastegate issues on modern 718 variants, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Porsche Boxster specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Porsche Boxster Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Porsche Boxster and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.description: All Porsche Boxster models use petrol flat-six or flat-four engines; naturally aspirated units dominate classic 986/987 generations while modern 718 variants feature turbocharged displacement.
items.descriptor: All Porsche Boxster models use petrol flat-six or flat-four engines; naturally aspirated units dominate classic 986/987 generations while modern 718 variants feature turbocharged displacement.

engineYears.h2: Porsche Boxster Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Boxster's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Boxster launched with the 2.5L M96.20 flat-six, establishing the model's reputation but introducing the IMS bearing vulnerability that would define replacement demand.
keyChanges: Porsche Boxster (986) launched with mid-engine flat-six architecture
Tiptronic automatic option added for broader appeal
knownFor: Smooth, responsive naturally aspirated flat-six character
IMS bearing vulnerability emerges as key long-term concern
Strong enthusiast following drives sustained replacement demand
Early examples now entering classic territory with rising values
years.cta: Get quotes for 1997-1999 Porsche Boxster replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche Boxster 2.5 engine replacement • M96.20 IMS bearing fix • Boxster 2.5 rebuilt engine UK

years.description: The 986 range expanded with the 2.7L M96.22 and performance S 3.2 M96.21, increasing power but retaining the IMS bearing design that drives UK replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: 2.7L M96.22 introduced as new entry-level engine
Boxster S launched with 3.2L M96.21, 250-260hp output
Facelift updates for 2003-2004 with revised M96.23/M96.24 codes
knownFor: S 3.2 becomes most-searched variant for engine replacement in UK
IMS bearing failure risk peaks on high-mileage M96.21/M96.24 units
Cylinder bore scoring emerges as secondary failure pattern on S models
Strong aftermarket support for preventative IMS upgrades
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2004 Porsche Boxster replacement engines
years.ticker: Boxster S 3.2 engine replacement • M96.21 IMS bearing failure • Boxster 2.7 bore scoring repair

years.description: The all-new 987 generation introduced the revised M97 engine family with improved architecture but timing chain tensioner wear emerges as the new replacement trigger for UK owners.
keyChanges: All-new 987 platform with updated styling and chassis
M97.21 3.4L flat-six introduced for Boxster S, 295hp output
Revised timing system but plastic tensioner guides remain a vulnerability
knownFor: Improved refinement and power over 986 generation
Timing chain tensioner wear becomes dominant failure pattern
Rear main seal leaks documented on higher-mileage examples
Strong demand for rebuilt M97.21 units in UK marketplace
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2008 Porsche Boxster replacement engines
years.ticker: Boxster S 3.4 engine replacement • M97.21 timing chain fix • Boxster 987 rebuilt engine UK

years.description: The 987.2 update introduced direct injection across the range plus limited-run GTS and Spyder variants, improving efficiency but adding carbon buildup as a new consideration for replacement buyers.
keyChanges: Direct injection introduced on M97.20 2.9L and M97.22 3.4L GTS
Boxster GTS launched with 303hp M97.22, sport-tuned components
Boxster Spyder limited edition with 320hp M97.24, highest-output naturally aspirated Boxster engine
knownFor: Direct injection improves efficiency but introduces carbon buildup concerns
GTS and Spyder command premium replacement pricing due to rarity
Timing chain tensioner wear remains relevant for M97-family engines
Strong collector interest in Spyder drives specialist rebuild demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2012 Porsche Boxster replacement engines
years.ticker: Boxster GTS engine replacement • Boxster Spyder rebuilt engine • M97 direct injection carbon fix

years.description: The 718 generation marked a radical shift to turbocharged flat-four engines, delivering strong performance but introducing new failure patterns around turbo components for UK replacement buyers to consider.
keyChanges: 718 Boxster launched with turbocharged MA1.22 2.0L flat-four, 300hp
718 Boxster S introduced with MA1.23 2.5L turbo, 350hp output
PDK dual-clutch transmission standard on most variants
popularModels: Porsche 718 Boxster GTS 4.0 (note: uses different naturally aspirated engine)
knownFor: Turbocharged performance delivers strong torque but new failure patterns
Wastegate rattle on early-production turbos is common replacement trigger
Direct injection carbon buildup remains a consideration on higher mileage
Premium pricing for rebuilt MA1-series engines reflects turbo complexity
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2020 Porsche Boxster replacement engines
years.ticker: 718 Boxster turbo engine replacement • MA1.22 wastegate rattle fix • 718 Boxster S rebuilt engine UK

faq.h2: Porsche Boxster Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Porsche Boxster engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Porsche Boxster engine prices range from £2,800 for entry 2.5L units to £11,500 for rare Spyder 3.8L engines, with supply-only pricing; supply & fit adds £800-£1,800 depending on location and workshop.
keyPoints: Entry 2.5L M96.20 rebuilt: £2,800-£4,500 supply only
Performance S 3.2 M96.21 rebuilt: £4,200-£7,800 supply only
Modern 718 turbo MA1.22 rebuilt: £5,500-£9,200 supply only
Supply & fit service adds £800-£1,800 to engine price
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Porsche Boxster engine prices for your variant

items.question: What is the most reliable Porsche Boxster engine code?
items.answer: The M97.20 2.9L and later M97-family engines (2009-2012) show improved reliability over earlier M96 units, with direct injection reducing some failure patterns though carbon buildup requires attention on higher mileage.
keyPoints: M97.20 2.9L (2009-2012) shows fewer IMS-related failures
Direct injection improves efficiency but requires carbon management
Later 987.2 models benefit from revised timing components
Regular servicing remains critical regardless of engine code
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable M97 Porsche Boxster replacement engines

items.question: Can I replace my Porsche Boxster engine with a different size?
items.answer: Engine swaps between Boxster variants are technically possible but require significant modification to ECU, wiring and ancillaries; most UK owners opt for direct like-for-like replacement to ensure MOT compliance and insurance validity.
keyPoints: Like-for-like replacement ensures MOT and insurance compliance
ECU and wiring modifications needed for cross-displacement swaps
Specialist knowledge required for non-standard installations
Direct replacement offers best value and reliability for most owners
items.cta: Compare like-for-like Porsche Boxster engine replacement options

items.question: How long does a Porsche Boxster engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 3-7 working days from UK specialists; full supply & fit service requires 2-4 days workshop time depending on variant complexity and ancillary work required.
keyPoints: Supply-only delivery: 3-7 working days typical UK timeframe
Supply & fit workshop time: 2-4 days depending on variant
Additional time needed if ancillary components require replacement
Specialist workshops can often prioritise urgent replacement requests
items.cta: Find Porsche Boxster engine supply & fit deals with fast turnaround

items.question: What warranty comes with a Porsche Boxster replacement engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt and reconditioned Porsche Boxster engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty; used engines typically carry shorter 3-6 month coverage depending on supplier terms.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/reconditioned: minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Used engines: typically 3-6 month coverage, verify before purchase
Warranty covers parts and labour for qualifying failures
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare Porsche Boxster engine warranty options from UK specialists

items.question: How do I find my Porsche Boxster engine code?
items.answer: Your Porsche Boxster engine code is stamped on the engine block near the oil filter housing and listed on your V5C registration document; enter your registration above for instant DVLA-verified code identification.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near oil filter housing
V5C registration document lists engine code in section D.2
VIN decoder tools can cross-reference code by chassis number
DVLA lookup via registration provides instant verified identification
items.cta: Get instant Porsche Boxster engine code identification via registration

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Porsche Boxster engine?
items.answer: For Boxsters in good overall condition with desirable variants like S 3.2 or GTS, engine replacement typically offers better value than purchasing a replacement vehicle, especially when using rebuilt units with upgraded components.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost often 30-50% below equivalent replacement car
Upgraded components on rebuilt units address original failure points
Desirable variants (S, GTS, Spyder) retain strong residual values
Full service history and recent maintenance improve replacement value case
items.cta: Get a tailored quote to assess your Porsche Boxster replacement value

items.question: Can I fit a Porsche Boxster engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible for experienced mechanics, Porsche Boxster engine replacement requires specialist tools, alignment equipment and ECU coding knowledge; most UK owners achieve better value using vetted specialist supply & fit services.
keyPoints: Specialist tools required for gearbox removal and engine alignment
ECU coding and adaptation needed post-installation for optimal operation
Warranty coverage typically voided by non-specialist installation
Vetted specialist fitting ensures warranty validity and reliability
items.cta: Find vetted Porsche Boxster engine fitting specialists near you

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and used Porsche Boxster engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt Porsche Boxster engines undergo comprehensive refurbishment with upgraded components addressing known failures and include 12-month warranty; used engines are donor units with verified mileage but shorter warranty and no component upgrades.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: upgraded IMS bearings, new seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
Used: verified mileage donor unit, shorter warranty, no component upgrades
Rebuilt offers better long-term value for high-mileage or performance variants
Used suitable for budget-conscious owners with lower-mileage expectations
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Porsche Boxster engine options and pricing

items.question: Do Porsche Boxster replacement engines come with ECU?
items.answer: Most rebuilt and reconditioned Porsche Boxster engines are supplied without ECU; your existing ECU can typically be reused after adaptation, though some specialists offer ECU-inclusive packages for complex variants or damaged donor vehicles.
keyPoints: Standard supply: engine only, your existing ECU reused after adaptation
ECU adaptation required post-installation for optimal operation
Some specialists offer ECU-inclusive packages for complex variants
Confirm ECU arrangement with supplier before ordering to avoid delays
items.cta: Enquire about Porsche Boxster engine and ECU compatibility options
